title: Pichia pastoris transformation through electroporation
author: Igem Dusseldorf
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8heht3e
materials: Tranfer mixture to ice-cold 0.2 cm electroporation cuvette

steps.title: Prior to transformation plasmid DNA containing...
steps.instruction: Prior to transformation plasmid DNA containing the gene(s) of interest was linearized in restriction digests using an enzyme that only cuts once following the manufacturers manual !

steps.title: Mix approx
steps.instruction: Mix approx. 150 ng of linearized vector DNA with electrocompetent Pichia cells

steps.title: Electroporate using a BioRad MicroPulser with...
steps.instruction: Electroporate using a BioRad MicroPulser with a charging voltage of 1.5 kV

steps.title: Immediately add 1 mL of 1:1...
steps.instruction: Immediately add 1 mL of 1:1 1 M sorbitol and YPD (v/v) to recover the cells

steps.title: Transfer mixture to a plastic culture...
steps.instruction: Transfer mixture to a plastic culture tube and incubated at 30°C for 1 hour at 225 rpm.

steps.title: Centrifuge culture tube at 3
steps.instruction: Centrifuge culture tube at 3.000 x g for 5 min to pellet the cells

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in 200 μL YPD media
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 200 μL YPD media

steps.title: Plate out cells on YPDS plates...
steps.instruction: Plate out cells on YPDS plates containing desired antibiotic and incubated at 30°C for 3 days
